patch.value: History and git growth policy automation: compaction cadence, gc scheduling, and archive guidance for million-event repositories

Growth-policy child of pm-9rxu: hash-chained JSONL streams and 1M+ .toon files make the git repo itself the long-term bottleneck. Builds on existing history-compact/gc primitives (pm-o2kc closed children) — automation + policy only, no new rewrite semantics.

Documented + tooled policy for repo growth per ADR pm-bl8x: history-compact/gc thresholds that pm health surfaces as advisories when exceeded; measured git operation cost (status/add/commit) at 1M items with recommendations applied; optional closed-item archive tier decision implemented or explicitly rejected in the ADR; replay integrity preserved (existing history-compact guarantees)
